---
title: "2.5 Compound Boolean Expressions"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Compound Conditions

### One branch can depend on more than one test

Sometimes a single Boolean test is not enough.

::: {.tight-list}
- both conditions must be true
- at least one condition must be true
- one condition must be false
:::

This topic combines simple Boolean expressions into <span class="term">compound Boolean expressions</span>.

## `&&`, `||`, and `!`

### The three core logical operators

```java
if (cleanedRoom && didHomework)
if (walking || carIsAvailable)
if (!homeworkDone)
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `&&` means <span class="term">and</span>
- `||` means <span class="term">or</span>
- `!` means <span class="term">not</span>
:::

In Java, `||` is inclusive OR, so it is true when either side is true or when both sides are true.

## Common Uses with Numbers

### `or` often checks for error ranges, `and` often checks valid ranges

```java
score < 0 || score > 100
score >= 0 && score <= 100
```

::: {.tight-list}
- use `||` when a value can be invalid on either end
- use `&&` when a value must stay inside a range
- use parentheses when the grouping is not obvious
:::

## Truth Tables

### A truth table shows every possible combination

For two Boolean values `P` and `Q`:

::: {.tight-list}
- `P && Q` is only true when <span class="mark">both</span> are true
- `P || Q` is true when <span class="mark">either</span> one is true
- `!P` flips true to false and false to true
:::

Truth tables are the cleanest way to explain why a compound condition works.

## Short Circuit Evaluation

### Java sometimes skips the second test

```java
if (x > 0 && (y / x) == 3)
if (x == 0 || (y / x) == 3)
```

::: {.tight-list}
- with `&&`, if the left side is false, Java stops
- with `||`, if the left side is true, Java stops
- this is called <span class="term">short circuit evaluation</span>
- short circuiting can prevent errors such as division by zero
:::

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- tracing `&&`, `||`, and `!` in simple conditionals
- range checks with numeric examples
- truth-table completion and branch prediction
- short-circuit reasoning with divide-by-zero cases
- <span class="term">2.5.4 Coding Challenge: Truth Tables POGIL</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- distinguish `&&`, `||`, and `!`
- explain the difference between range checks and error checks
- use truth-table reasoning for compound conditions
- explain Java short circuit evaluation
- recognize why short circuiting can avoid runtime errors
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
